sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me systematically map all blue block positions and their effects.
# First, let me identify all blue blocks in the current frame.

seg = current_frame.segmentation
blue_blocks = []
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'b':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        blue_blocks.append((min(rows), min(cols), max(rows), max(cols)))
print("Blue blocks:")
for bb in blue_blocks:
    print(f"  ({bb[0]},{bb[1]})-({bb[2]},{bb[3]})")

# Let me also check the white columns
white_blocks = []
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] > 10:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        white_blocks.append((min(rows), min(cols), max(rows), max(cols)))
print("\nWhite columns:")
for wb in white_blocks:
    print(f"  ({wb[0]},{wb[1]})-({wb[2]},{wb[3]})")

# Let me try clicking on blue blocks that I haven't tested yet.
# I've tested: (56,12), (56,16), (56,24), (56,28), (56,34), (56,38), (56,46), (56,50)
# I haven't tested: (56,20), (56,22), (56,30), (56,32), (56,40), (56,42), (56,44), (56,52), (56,54), (56,56)
# But (56,20) only changed the HUD bar.

# Let me try clicking on the blue blocks that are at the edges of the white columns.
# The white columns are at cols 6-13, 18-23, 28-35, 38-47, 50-57.
# The blue blocks are at cols 12-13, 16-17, 24-25, 28-29, 34-35, 38-39, 46-47, 50-51.

# Let me try clicking on (56,30) which is in the third white column (cols 28-35).
print("\nTrying (56,30):")
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 56, 'col': 30}], intent='solve')
print(f"  state={state_graph.current}, completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")
print(f"  gameplay_changed={last_action_result.get('gameplay_changed')}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['c', 'N', 'p', 'Y'] and n['pixels'] <= 6:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  {n['color']} at ({min(rows)},{min(cols)})-({max(rows)},{max(cols)})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
